title: crotalaria pycnostachya subsp. donaldsonii
intro: Crotalaria pycnostachya subsp. donaldsonii, also known as Donaldson's rattlebox, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which can help to treat a range of health issues. The health benefits of Crotalaria pycnostachya subsp. donaldsonii include its potential to reduce fever, relieve pain, and treat respiratory infections, as well as its antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In traditional herbal preparations, Crotalaria pycnostachya subsp. donaldsonii is often made into a tea or infusion to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
